recipes.title: One machine per entity
recipes.problem: Don't try to fit ten orders into one machine. Spin up a fresh instance per row, and let each one run its own lifecycle independently.

recipes.title: Subscribe from React
recipes.problem: Render off the machine's state with a tiny hook. The component re-renders on every transition; you never call setState yourself.

recipes.title: Side effects on entry
recipes.problem: Fire a side effect every time the machine arrives at a state — log it, persist it, page someone — without scattering checks across your call sites.

recipes.title: Transition logging
recipes.problem: Tap every transition with a single line. Useful in dev, essential in production for replaying user sessions.

recipes.title: Payload-aware actions
recipes.problem: Most actions carry data — a form submission, a server response, a user id. Pass it through with the transition and read it in the hook.

recipes.title: Acyclic flow
recipes.problem: A one-way pipeline with a terminal state. Once you've shipped, you've shipped — there's no going back.

recipes.title: Auth flow
recipes.problem: Sign-in, sign-out, and session-expired collapsed into a single machine. The login form, the home screen, and the silent-refresh worker all read the same `state()`.

recipes.title: Optional branch
recipes.problem: A pipeline with one optional fork — review-then-publish, or skip-and-publish. The branch lives in the graph, not in a flag you have to remember to set.

recipes.title: Cycle
recipes.problem: A linear loop with one shared verb. Every state is reachable from every other state, in order, by repeatedly firing the same action.

recipes.title: Data fetch
recipes.problem: The four-state shape every data hook actually has: idle / loading / ready / failed. Once you write it down as a machine, the impossible loading-and-error states stop happening.

recipes.title: Toggle
recipes.problem: Two states, one verb. The smallest useful machine — and the one you reach for whenever a boolean has started growing edge cases.

recipes.title: Fetch with retry
recipes.problem: A request that can succeed, fail, or be retried — without a tangle of nested if-statements. The graph names every legal transition; the calling code stays flat.

recipes.title: Multi-step form
recipes.problem: Wizard, checkout, onboarding. Forward and back navigation, with the submit button only legal on the last step.

recipes.title: Optimistic UI
recipes.problem: Show success immediately, roll back on failure. The machine carries the optimistic state explicitly, so the rollback path is just another arrow.

recipes.title: Testing transitions
recipes.problem: Walk every legal path with a tiny generator. Assert that illegal actions are refused. Your test suite gets graph coverage for free.

recipes.title: Jest × Webpack × React
recipes.problem: The classic CRA-shaped stack. Same machine tests, just run under Jest with `jsdom` and `babel-jest`.

recipes.title: Vitest × Vite × React
recipes.problem: Wire jssm machines into a Vitest suite that runs against a Vite-powered React app. State transitions assert as plain function calls; React renders as a snapshot of `state()`.

recipes.title: Vitest × Vite × Vue
recipes.problem: Vue 3 + `@vue/test-utils` against a jssm machine. The store-shaped pattern: a single machine drives `<script setup>` reactivity through a tiny ref wrapper.
